programmes.show_description: Record On: The Specials - A Message to You: Career-spanning documentary from the band's formation to later albums Encore and Protest Songs.
Andi Oliver and Elizabeth Day host as members of Focus Group book club from Wiltshire meet podcaster and comedian Adam Buxton to discuss his memoir. (2 of 6)
Art's Most Satanic: Waldemar goes looking for the Devil, the ultimate shape-shifter, who keeps popping up in art in a wicked assortment of disguises. (3 of 3)
The art collection of late pop superstar George Michael goes under the hammer at Christie's in an auction featuring works by Tracey Emin, Damien Hirst and Bridget Riley.
A rare painting by Christopher Nevinson comes to Sotheby's, while bidders scramble for the last painting by Winston Churchill - of his beloved goldfish pool at Chartwell. (S10, ep 2)
Salvator Mundi, the long-lost Leonardo da Vinci painting of Jesus Christ commissioned by King Louis XII, is set to break all records at auction at Christie's in New York. (S10, ep 3)
Over five years following the devastating fire, France's renowned Notre-Dame de Paris has once again opened its doors, marked by a glamorous concert to honour its restoration.
Blaze of Colour: Evening sky sets in, giving way to a perfect glow. Bob makes a black canvas come to life with his magical brush strokes. (S6, ep 13)
Art competition in which amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours. (S2, ep 11)
Art competition in which amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours. (S2, ep 12)
Royal Jelly: Herbert Wise stars in this twisted tale. Mabel's baby is sick and her bee-obsessed husband thinks he has found a remedy. Will he get the baby buzzing back to health? (S2, ep 4)
Poison: Andrew Ray stars in Roald Dahl's twisted tale. A poisonous snake keeps a teacher prisoner in India. How can he escape from its evil glare? (S2, ep 5)
Decoy: Gil Larkin is suspected of murder when Mona Cameron's allegedly abusive husband is shot dead in front of him. (S1, ep 37)
The Creeper: A frightened housewife is alone in her apartment when the realisation sets in that just about anyone could be the unknown killer on the loose. (S1, ep 38)
A look at the life of the celebrated filmmaker of hits Walkabout, Don't Look Now and Bad Timing. His unique style made him a highly influential filmmaker. (S5, ep 5)
A look at the life and legendary career of the original James Bond - Academy Award-winning Sean Connery. (S10, ep 9)
The Hitch-Hiker: Rod Taylor stars in Roald Dahl's twisted tale. When a driver picks up a hitch-hiker in his car, his journey turns into a thrilling ride. (S2, ep 6)
Genesis and Catastrophe: A Roald Dahl story with a twist. A pregnant lady is worried that she may miscarry for the fourth time. Can her doctor reassure her? (S2, ep 7)
Focusing on the art and artists within Tate Britain's collection, featuring various art historians and cultural figures exploring different themes and artists. (1 of 2)
New. The Scream: More than a painting, The Scream provides a glimpse into Edvard Munch's mind. Learn his struggles with anxiety and the deterioration of his works over time. (S1, ep 1)
New. Starry Night: Trace the tragedy of Van Gogh's short life, the brilliance born from pain and the legacy of a genius whose masterpiece continues to captivate and inspire. (S1, ep 2)
Remembering the life of David Lynch, the Academy Award-winning director behind Mulholland Drive, Blue Velvet and The Elephant Man, whose visionary work reshaped cinema. (S5, ep 6)
A look at the life of the late Robin Williams, the Academy Award-winning star of much-loved films such as Good Will Hunting, Jumanji and Mrs Doubtfire. (S10, ep 10)
Mr Botibol's First Love: Jake Weston stars in this twisted story. A jaded businessman sells his company and attempts to change his life for the better by buying a strange piano. (S2, ep 8)
The Umbrella Man: John Mills stars in this story with a twist. A con man arrives in town and starts his scam. Michael Gambon guest stars. (S2, ep 9)
Momentum: Richard Paine decides to steal money from his boss that he has owed him for months, and it turns into an unnecessary and ironic murder. (S1, ep 39)
Wet Saturday: Mr Princey's daughter has just murdered the schoolmaster. It's up to Mr Princey to frame someone else for her crime. (S2, ep 1)
A look at the life and career of the Hollywood actor, whose roles in Jaws, All That Jazz and The French Connection catapulted him to stardom. (S12, ep 7)

programmes.show_name: Classic Movies: The Story Of The Graduate
programmes.show_description: Movie critic Ian Nathan examines the qualities that made this 1967 romantic comedy drama starring Dustin Hoffman an all-time classic. (S1, ep 4)
A wandering gunfighter plays two rival families against each other in a town torn apart by greed, pride and revenge. (1964)
A look at the life and career of the Hollywood filmmaker, famous for directing films such as the Academy Award-nominated Blossoms in the Dust and Thirty Seconds Over Tokyo. (S5, ep 8)
An insightful film documenting the life of actor and martial artist Bruce Lee, with insight from his family shedding light on who he really was. Pete McCormack (Facing Ali) directs.
Feature documentary capturing Katharine Hepburn's tremendous spirit and determination, combining new and archive footage to tell the story of a true Hollywood icon.
The Courtauld Institute Of Art: A series of fascinating shorts taking you deeper into the world of art and the people behind it.
The skeletons of a woolly mammoth family - found on a building site in Siberia in 2002 - are auctioned in Sussex. Meanwhile, American art goes under the hammer in New York. (S10, ep 4)
Twenty-five years after her death, some of Audrey Hepburn's possessions come up for sale at Christie - and the public interest is intense. (S10, ep 5)
Violin sensation Andre Rieu and his Johann Strauss Orchestra take to the stage in the Irish capital for a lively and enchanting performance of his best-loved music.

programmes.show_name: The Brontes by Anita Rani: Sisters of Disruption
programmes.show_description: Anita Rani explores the Brontë sisters and their enduring influence, tracking the impact Anne, Charlotte and Emily have had on millions of readers.
A look at the life of the inspirational filmmaker, who won the first-ever Academy Award for Writing Original Screenplay for his comedy film The Great McGinty. (S5, ep 7)
A look at the life of legendary two-time Academy Award winner Robert De Niro, star of The Godfather Part II, Taxi Driver and Goodfellas. (S10, ep 11)
The Flypaper: Lorna Charles and Alfred Burke star in this twisted tale. With a 12 year-old found dead and a killer perhaps on the loose, a young girl becomes paranoid. (S2, ep 10)
The Orderly World of Mr Appleby: Robert Lang stars in this tale with a twist. A man married three times might be lucky for the fourth time when he meets a new interest. (S2, ep 11)
Fog Closing In: When her husband goes away on a business trip, Mary Summers is all alone in her house when an escaped patient breaks in. (S2, ep 2)
De Mortuis: Professor Rankin's buddies Wally and Bud stop by to take him fishing. Suspicions rise when they find him in his cellar, filling in a hole with cement. (S2, ep 3)
1986: Elbow frontman Guy Garvey lifts the lid on two decades of TV gold. Here, he takes you through 1986, reflecting on the year's defining musical performances. (3 of 5)
Motorhead's fourth album was loud, fast and heavy. Slash, Lars Ulrich, Dave Brock and the band discuss its creation and influence. Contains strong language and adult themes.
Features Lemmy and his band of metal warriors shaking Munich's Deutsches Museum to its foundations with live renditions of their music.
The band's most commercially successful line-up, featuring Ritchie Blackmore, Ian Gillan, Roger Glover, Jon Lord and Ian Paice, reunite in 1984 for an electrifying concert in Melbourne.
